properties.Notes: E 1/2 of section 3 plus approx. 40 acres of road allowances surrendered in 1914. NW corner of section 2 surrendered in 1919, the rest by 1933 for the Church of Englang/Gordon School.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1876. Boundaries adjusted in 1881. West half of section 4 twp 27 range 17 patended to Church of England. Other portions surrendered to the CofE and Gordon School in 1914, 1919, and 1933.

properties.Notes: Surrendered to be sold for the purchase of a new engine
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey conducted for three bands. 1883 survey notes it was "abandoned." Piapot's band left in 1883. Reserve resurveyed in 1885, reduced in size to 73.21 sq. miles. 9 sections along south boundary surrendered in 1905.

properties.Notes: SW section surrendered to be sold to settlers. North section surrendered for Cowessess boarding school.
properties.NOTES_1: O'Soup's and followers initially settled on N side of Qu'appelle River, moved to the south side, provided 63 sq. mi., expanded to 78 sq. mi. in 1883. Settlers petitioned for surrender. 41% of land surrendered in 1907. 1908 school area surrendered.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for settlement of Lakeview (Regina Beach) in 1919
properties.NOTES_1: Surveyed in 1885 as a fishing station for multiple reserves. 219.7 acres surrendered in 1919 for railway and settlement of Lakeview (Regina Beach). Remaining 1207.5 acres shared by multiple nations.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale and settlement of Kamsack
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1877, 35 families. CNR pursued surrender in 1904, 1905, and 1907 for settlement of Kamsack and settler farming in the area. More sections surrendered in 1913, no immediate buyers, land returned by 1916/17.

properties.Notes: Partially surrendered to be sold to settlers
properties.NOTES_1: Settlers petitioned for surrender of reserve lands from 1885. 33281 acres surrendered in 1907.

properties.Notes: Sold to Soldier Settlement Board in 1919, part of the soldier settlement act.
properties.NOTES_1: Inital reserve alloted on north side of river in 1877, before being moved to the south side. Reserve surveyed in 1880. Refused Broadview petitioned surrender in 1907. Soldier Settlement Board surrender in 1919.

properties.Notes: Sold to the Soldier Settlement Board as part of the Soldier Settlement Act
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey in 1876 laid out 24960 acres, 1889 map suggests 27200 acres. Portions surrendered in 1919 as part of the Soldier Settlement Act, reducing acreage to 19125 as documented in the 1972 schedule.

properties.Notes: Surrendered to be sold in 1909, $85 paid to each individual.
properties.NOTES_1: initial survey 1884. Confusing extensions made in the 1890s, seemingly the 1884 survey stuck. In 1909, 7600 acres "unanimously" surrendered to be sold.

properties.Notes: Illegal surrender of 12461 acres in 1928.
properties.NOTES_1: initial survey conducted in late 1880, no map until 1885. Illegal surrender in 1928 of 12461 acres.

properties.Notes: Amalgamated with Sakimay and subsequently surrendered in 1907 on dubious grounds.
properties.NOTES_1: initial survey 1884.  Informally amalgamated with Sakimay (Zagime Anishinabek) in 1887. 1907 Graham pursues surrender, families forced off the land in 1921 with residences burned.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1881. Southern portion swapped in resurvey 1881. Suggestion of 17600 acres surrendered in 1909 report. 1913 schedule lists 17957.2 (including "For Balgonie Road 23.4 acres").

properties.Notes: Surrendered in 1920.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1884. Boundary confusion with George Gordon's #86, corrected in 1900. 158.18 acres surrendered in 1906 for GTR. 160 acres surrendered for Lestock settlement in 1910. 7485 acres in 1920.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for town plot of Lestock and "right of way" for Grand Trunck Railroad.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1884. Boundary confusion with George Gordon's #86, corrected in 1900. 158.18 acres surrendered in 1906 for GTR. 160 acres surrendered for Lestock settlement in 1910. 7485 acres in 1920.

properties.Notes: Surrendered in 1901. Moved to White Bear.
properties.NOTES_1: Amalgamated with Pheasant's Rump #68 and surrendered agreement was signed in 1901. No mention of a vote. Moved to White Bear # 70. Total surrender was pursued by Commissioner Laird due to proximity to the CPR railing and decent soil for settlers.

properties.Notes: Illegally surrendered in 1901. Moved to White Bear.
properties.NOTES_1: Amalgamated with Ocean Man #69 and "surrendered" in 1901. Likely not involved surrender negotiations. Moved to White Bear # 70. Total surrender was pursued by Commissioner Laird due to proximity to the CPR railing and settler farming opportunities.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale in Soldier Settlement
properties.NOTES_1: Abandoned initial reserve in 1884, moved next Muscowepetung #80 in Qu'Appelle Valley in 1884. Sureveyed in 1885. Partial surrender of 2180 acres in 1918, mostly purchased by the Soldier Settlement Board. Another surrender in 1919 of 16,318 acres.

properties.Notes: Surrendered, no record in DIA reports
properties.NOTES_1: Abandoned initial reserve in 1884, moved next Muscowepetung #80 in Qu'Appelle Valley in 1884. Sureveyed in 1885. Partial surrender of 2180 acres in 1918, mostly purchased by the Soldier Settlement Board. Another surrender in 1919 of 16,318 acres.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale in 1906, 16077 acres
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey in 1876, subdivision of farmland survey in 1889. Pressured surrender for sale of 16,077 acres as pursued by local settlers in 1906.

properties.Notes: Surrendered as part of an exchange in 1927. This section seemingly not returned.
properties.NOTES_1: See Shoal Lake 28. Surveyed in 1894, several additions/exchanges made according to the 1894 survey map with annotations to 1927, addition in 1906, another in 1913 and 1914. Supposed exchange of 640 acres "surrendered for exchange" in 1927.

properties.Notes: Purchased by Dept. 1925, Seemingly returned before 1972.
properties.NOTES_1: Did not sign Treaty 4 but are protected under the Indian Act. Initial survey in 1881. Additions made of up to 560 acres in 1902/3. SE 6 22 14 W2 purchased by "Dept." 144 acres, seemingly returned prior to 1972 schedule.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey set out in 1876 near Swan River, settled there for some time. Moved to Fort Pelly area in 1882. New survey in 1884 at 38 sq. miles. 11,500 acres surrendered for sale in 1909.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale and settlement of Kylemore.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed adhesion to Treaty 4 in 1876. Moved into Touchwood Hills area late 1870s. Intimated locations for reserves near Fishing Lake and Nut Lake. Surveyed in 1881. CPR pursued surrenders of 1904 and 1907, latter for settlement.

properties.Notes: Illegal transfer of members and illegal total surrender as identified in ICC 2005
properties.GRRD_CD_NA: Chakastaypasin Band of the Cree Nation
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Partially surveyed 1878. Following 1885 Resistance, many members moved to other reserves. Pressure increased to open up reserve land for settler use. Members transferred to Cumberland No. 100A, total surrender by OIC in 1897.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Initially within Treaty 5. Some members from Cumberland House #20 moved to Fort à la Corne. #100A (65 sq. mi.) surveyed in 1887 next to James Smith #100. Amalgamated in 1902 with James Smith #100 following surrender of 22,080 acres.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale to Soldier Resettlement Board
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Unclear on date of "uptake" of reserve. Initially surveyed in 1881. Subdivision survey in 1892. 8960 acres surrendered for sale to soldier resettlement board in 1919. Unsurrendered portion subdivided 1922.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for Soldier Settlement Board
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Subdivision survery as part of severalty policy in 1891. Right of way surrender of 14.14 acres for CNR. 1607 acres surrendered in 1911, another 5028 acres in 1917, and 11520 in 1919 for soldier settlement.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for fencing materials and farming machinery
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Subdivision survery as part of severalty policy in 1891. Right of way surrender of 14.14 acres for CNR. 1607 acres surrendered in 1911, another 5028 acres in 1917, and 11520 in 1919 for soldier settlement.

properties.Notes: Land outside fenced area surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Subdivision survery as part of severalty policy in 1891. Right of way surrender of 14.14 acres for CNR. 1607 acres surrendered in 1911, another 5028 acres in 1917, and 11520 in 1919 for soldier settlement.

properties.Notes: Transfer to CPR
properties.NOTES_1: Signed an adhesion to Treaty 6 in 1878. Initial survey in 1881, little uptake of reserve until late 1880s. Land transferred to the CPR 1932, additional land sold to the town of Meadow Lake 1959. Specific claim about 1932 railway expropriation resolved.

properties.Notes: No uptake of reserve, returned to DIA in 1897 without acquiring surrender.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Reserve surveyed 1879. No significant uptake according to DIA. 1897, reserve land "restored" to the departmnet. Deeded out to settlers. Marked as abandoned in 1877. No proper surrender acquisition.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Signed adhesion to Treaty 4 in 1877. Reserve surveyed adjacent to #109 Mosquito in 1884. 14,532 acres surrednered for sale in 1905.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Hay land provided to #112 Moosomin and #115 Thunderchild in 1887. 640 acres surrendered 1909, 640 acres retained.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Provided hay lands in 1887 to be shared with #115 Thunderchild. Settlers and government pursued surrender despute continuous rejections by Moosomin band. Total surrender acquired in 1909 of both #112 and #112A. #112B surveyed as a replacement.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Surveyed in 1884, unsure of usage or uptake. No population data outside of total Thunderchild population count. Total surrender 1908.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Signed an adhesion to Treaty 6, 1879. Initial survey 1881. 115A added in 1884, 112A Hay Lands added in 1887 (surrendered 640 acers in 1909). Entire reserve surrendered in 1908. Replaced by 115B.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for exchange for 118A
properties.NOTES_1: Signed an adhesion to Treaty 6, 1878. Proceeded to live by fishing and hunting for a number of years in the area near where their reserve was eventually surveyed in 1898. 980 acres surrendered for exchange for 118A in 1908.

properties.Notes: Sold to Soldier Settlement Board. Since been returned? No final sale? Unclear.
properties.NOTES_1: Created in 1908 from surrendered land from 118. entire reserve surrendered for sale to soldier settlement board. Has since been returned? Unsure when. Counted in 1972 schedule.

properties.Notes: Abandoned in 1886 when the majority Métis residents left treaty and took scrip. Officially surrendered 5376 acres in 1918.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 5 as part of "The Pas Band." Reserved surveyed in 1882. 1913 schedule suggests 5736 acres were surrendered, likely a typo. Total surrender in 1918, replaced by #27A in 1912 to address treaty land deficit.

properties.Notes: Estimated land surrender boundaries. acreage surrendered is within 100 acres of actual acreage difference between 1902 and 1913 schedules.
properties.NOTES_1: In 1883, 4025 acres were surveyed, however, 11040 acres were to be alloted to the 345 band members. Many left between 1883 and 1885. Surrender in 1902 of 540 acres. Rough georeference to arrive at the land surrender location.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for sale
properties.NOTES_1: Surveyed in 1905 as part of Swan Lake No. 7 in Treaty 1/Manitoba to make up for survey errors and deficient treaty land provision there in 1876. Surrendered for sale in 1909.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for Soldier Settlement
properties.NOTES_1: Present at treaty negotiations in 1870s, never took treaty. Temporary reserve created in 1910. Seemingly no consultation for surrender in 1919 for soldier settlement. Government approved the reserve in 1930.

properties.Notes: Patented to the HBC sometime after the initial survey in 1887, returned to the department in 1927.
properties.GRRD_CD_NA: Little Pine First Nation and Lucky Man Cree Nation
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6 adhesion, 1879. Among others near Fort Walsh in early 1880s. Some of Lucky Man's followers were incorporated into Battleford bands. Two sections patented to HBC in reserve, later returned. Seemingly, no other surrenders.

properties.Notes: Grand Trunk Pacific Railway granted throughway by Order-in-Council.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed adhesion to Treaty 6 in 1878. Reserve initially surveyed in 1879, Chief Mosquito chose land adjacent to Red Pheasant #108 in Eagle Hills instead. GTR pursued surrender for "right of way".

properties.RSRVE_NAME: Grizzly Bear's Head and Lean Man
properties.Notes: Grand Trunk Pacific Railway granted throughway through Order-in-Council.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed adhesion to Treaty 4 in 1877. Reserve surveyed adjacent to #109 Mosquito in 1884. 14,532 acres surrednered for sale in 1905.

properties.Notes: Grand Trunk Pacific Railway granted throughway through Order-in-Council.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey in 1876, subdivision of farmland survey in 1889. Pressured surrender for sale of 16,077 acres as pursued by local settlers in 1906.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for Canadian Pacific Railway throughway.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed Treaty 6, 1876. Subdivision survery as part of severalty policy in 1891. Right of way surrender of 14.14 acres for CNR. 1607 acres surrendered in 1911, another 5028 acres in 1917, and 11520 in 1919 for soldier settlement.

properties.Notes: Surrendered for Grand Trunk Railway throughway.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1884. Boundary confusion with George Gordon's #86, corrected in 1900. 158.18 acres surrendered in 1906 for GTR. 160 acres surrendered for Lestock settlement in 1910. 7485 acres in 1920.

properties.Notes: E 1/2 of section 3 plus approx. 40 acres of road allowances surrendered in 1914. NW corner of section 2 surrendered in 1919, the rest by 1933 for the Church of Englang/Gordon School.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1876. Boundaries adjusted in 1881. West half of section 4 twp 27 range 17 patended to Church of England. Other portions surrendered to the CofE and Gordon School in 1914, 1919, and 1933.

properties.Notes: E 1/2 of section 3 plus approx. 40 acres of road allowances surrendered in 1914. NW corner of section 2 surrendered in 1919, the rest by 1933 for the Church of Englang/Gordon School.
properties.NOTES_1: Initial survey 1876. Boundaries adjusted in 1881. West half of section 4 twp 27 range 17 patended to Church of England. Other portions surrendered to the CofE and Gordon School in 1914, 1919, and 1933.

properties.Notes: "Right of Way" surrender for Canadian Northern Railway.
properties.NOTES_1: Signed adhesion to Treaty 4 in 1876. Moved into Touchwood Hills area late 1870s. Intimated locations for reserves near Fishing Lake and Nut Lake. Surveyed in 1881. CPR pursued surrenders of 1904 and 1907, latter for settlement.

properties.Notes: Road Allowance surrender.
properties.NOTES_1: Initially surveyed in 1881, overlapped St. Laurent Metis Settlement surveyed in 1878. Recognized in 1884 and resurveyed in 1888. Order in Council confirmed reserve in 1889. Surrender taken in 1895 for a road allowance.

properties.Notes: Southern portion was patented to the HBC prior to acceptance of the reserve in 1889. The 96 acres initially surveyed were reduced to 68.5.
properties.NOTES_1: provided access to Crooked Lake for fishing. Surrendered in totatily sometime after 1913.

properties.Notes: Road Allowance transfered to government without compensation.
properties.NOTES_1: provided access to Crooked Lake for fishing. Surrendered in totatily sometime after 1913.
